chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# Chapter 11: The Validation Framework
# ==============================================================================
# Module-level intent for the eleventh chapter of the IVD book. Opens Part III
# (Validation) by defining the scientific methodology for proving IVD works:
# five validation categories, experimental design, statistical rigor, and the
# framework that chapters 12–16 execute against.
#
# Reference: book/book_system_intent.yaml (system)
# Parent:    book_structure.part_3_validation.chapters[0]
# Source:    framework.md, ivd_system_intent.yaml, Part II project measurements
# Version: 1.0
# Created: 2026-02-09
# Status: Pending
# ==============================================================================

parent_intent: "../../../book_system_intent.yaml"

scope:
  level: "module"
  type: "chapter"
  granularity: "coarse"

# ==============================================================================
# INTENT: What should this chapter accomplish?
# ==============================================================================

intent:
  summary: "Define the validation framework that Part III uses to prove IVD works — five validation categories, experimental methodology, statistical rigor, and the baseline-vs-IVD controlled experiment design that chapters 12–16 execute."
  
  goal: |
    By the end of this chapter, the reader:
    1. Understands what IVD claims need proving and why evidence-based validation matters
    2. Knows the five validation categories: context efficiency, alignment accuracy, knowledge durability, recipe effectiveness, and meta-validation
    3. Can design a baseline-vs-IVD controlled experiment for any category
    4. Understands the statistical tools used: significance tests, confidence intervals, effect sizes
    5. Sees how Part II's per-project measurements (illustrative) become Part III's controlled experiments (rigorous)
    6. Has a clear roadmap: Ch11 defines methodology; Ch12–16 execute it and present results
    
    This is the opening chapter of Part III. The reader transitions from "I can build with IVD" (Part II) to "I can prove IVD works." Prose leads; evidence and methodology illustrations support. This chapter defines the framework, not the results.
  
  success_metric: |
    - Reader can list the five validation categories and explain what each measures
    - Reader understands the baseline-vs-IVD experimental design pattern
    - Reader knows what statistical significance means in this context and why it matters
    - Reader can distinguish between illustrative measurements (Part II) and controlled experiments (Part III)
    - Reader sees the validation framework as a system: categories reinforce each other
    - Chapter ends with a clear bridge to Chapter 12 (first experiment: context efficiency)
  
  stakeholders:
    - "IVD book author (manuscript)"
    - "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_3 purpose: prove every claim with data)"
    - "Part II chapters (measurements that Part III formalizes)"

# ==============================================================================
# DELIVERABLES: Required se

in this context and why it matters
    - Reader can distinguish between illustrative measurements (Part II) and controlled experiments (Part III)
    - Reader sees the validation framework as a system: categories reinforce each other
    - Chapter ends with a clear bridge to Chapter 12 (first experiment: context efficiency)
  
  stakeholders:
    - "IVD book author (manuscript)"
    - "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_3 purpose: prove every claim with data)"
    - "Part II chapters (measurements that Part III formalizes)"

# ==============================================================================
# DELIVERABLES: Required sections (from book_system_intent Ch11)
# ==============================================================================

deliverables:
  sections:
    - id: "11.1"
      title: "Claims to Validate"
      required_content:
        - "The transition from Part II to Part III: Part II built real projects and showed illustrative measurements; Part III proves those claims rigorously"
        - "Enumerate the specific IVD claims that need evidence: 85–95% context reduction, 40% faster onboarding, fewer iterations to alignment, knowledge survives context reset, recipes accelerate delivery"
        - "Why evidence matters: opinion-only books don't survive scrutiny; IVD's credibility depends on proving its claims with data (pattern: Code Complete's empirical chapters)"
        - "The standard each claim must meet: reproducible experiment, baseline comparison, statistical significance"
        - "What 'proving IVD works' means vs. what it doesn't: we prove measurable improvements under controlled conditions, not universal superiority in all contexts"
        - "Map each claim to its validation category (preview of 11.2–11.6): reader sees the full picture before diving into details"
    
    - id: "11.2"
      title: "Validation Category 1: Context Efficiency (Token Reduction)"
      required_content:
        - "What context efficiency measures: how much information an AI agent needs to understand a codebase with IVD intent artifacts vs. without"
        - "The hypothesis: IVD reduces the tokens an AI must process by 80–90% while maintaining or improving comprehension accuracy"
        - "How to measure it: token counting (tiktoken), comprehension accuracy tests (can the AI answer questions about the system correctly?)"
        - "Baseline design: AI reads all source files; IVD condition: AI reads only intent artifacts"
        - "Why this category matters: token reduction means faster responses, lower cost, and larger codebases within context windows"
        - "Preview of Chapter 12: this is where the controlled experiments and results live"
    
    - id: "11.3"
      title: "Validation Category 2: Alignment Accuracy (Iteration Reduction)"
      required_content:
        - "What alignment accuracy measures: how many iterations (human–AI exchanges) it takes to reach a correct, aligned implementation"
        - "The hypothesis: IVD reduces iterations to working implementation by 40–60% compared to ad-hoc prompting"
        - "How to measure it: count iterations to first passing test suite, count constraint violations caught by

er cost, and larger codebases within context windows"
        - "Preview of Chapter 12: this is where the controlled experiments and results live"
    
    - id: "11.3"
      title: "Validation Category 2: Alignment Accuracy (Iteration Reduction)"
      required_content:
        - "What alignment accuracy measures: how many iterations (human–AI exchanges) it takes to reach a correct, aligned implementation"
        - "The hypothesis: IVD reduces iterations to working implementation by 40–60% compared to ad-hoc prompting"
        - "How to measure it: count iterations to first passing test suite, count constraint violations caught by verification, count human corrections needed"
        - "Baseline design: same task described in natural language chat; IVD condition: same task with intent artifact provided"
        - "Why this category matters: fewer iterations means faster delivery and less frustration — the core promise of IVD"
        - "Preview of Chapter 13: controlled experiments comparing iteration counts across project types"
    
    - id: "11.4"
      title: "Validation Category 3: Knowledge Durability (Retention After Reset)"
      required_content:
        - "What knowledge durability measures: whether project understanding survives AI context resets (new session, different model, different developer)"
        - "The hypothesis: IVD intent artifacts preserve 85–95% of critical project knowledge across context resets, vs. near-zero retention without IVD"
        - "How to measure it: start new AI session, provide only intent artifacts (IVD) or only source code (baseline), measure task completion accuracy"
        - "Baseline design: new AI session with access to codebase only; IVD condition: new AI session with intent artifacts"
        - "Why this category matters: knowledge durability is IVD's unique differentiator — the 'knowledge capture' that survives team changes, model upgrades, and time"
        - "Preview of Chapter 14: experiments with multiple AI models, simulated team handoffs, time-gap retention"
    
    - id: "11.5"
      title: "Validation Category 4: Recipe Effectiveness (Time Savings)"
      required_content:
        - "What recipe effectiveness measures: whether IVD recipes (reusable intent templates) accelerate delivery compared to starting from scratch"
        - "The hypothesis: using an IVD recipe reduces time-to-working-implementation by 30–50% compared to building without a recipe"
        - "How to measure it: time from project start to first passing verification, lines of intent written, constraint coverage from recipe template"
        - "Baseline design: build the same project type without recipe guidance; IVD condition: start from the recipe template"
        - "Why this category matters: recipes are IVD's scaling mechanism — they encode best practices as reusable structures"
        - "Preview of Chapter 15: recipe-vs-no-recipe experiments across agent, workflow, and infrastructure patterns"
    
    - id: "11.6"
      title: "Validation Category 5: Meta-Validation (Self-Governance)"
      required_content:
        - "What meta-validation measures: whether IVD can validate itself — does the IVD system i

written, constraint coverage from recipe template"
        - "Baseline design: build the same project type without recipe guidance; IVD condition: start from the recipe template"
        - "Why this category matters: recipes are IVD's scaling mechanism — they encode best practices as reusable structures"
        - "Preview of Chapter 15: recipe-vs-no-recipe experiments across agent, workflow, and infrastructure patterns"
    
    - id: "11.6"
      title: "Validation Category 5: Meta-Validation (Self-Governance)"
      required_content:
        - "What meta-validation measures: whether IVD can validate itself — does the IVD system intent accurately govern the IVD framework?"
        - "The hypothesis: IVD's own artifacts (ivd_system_intent.yaml, book_system_intent.yaml) satisfy the same constraints they prescribe; the framework is internally consistent"
        - "How to measure it: apply IVD's verification principles to IVD's own artifacts — do constraints have tests? Do intent summaries match actual behavior? Is the hierarchy consistent?"
        - "Why meta-validation matters: a framework that can't govern itself has no credibility governing others; self-consistency is the ultimate test"
        - "The recursive argument: IVD validates IVD; if it fails its own standards, the framework has a fundamental flaw"
        - "Preview of Chapter 16: IVD applied to itself — a systematic meta-analysis"
    
    - id: "11.7"
      title: "Experimental Design and Methodology"
      required_content:
        - "The baseline-vs-IVD experimental pattern used across all five categories: control (no IVD) vs. treatment (with IVD), same tasks, same models, measured outcomes"
        - "Variables: independent (IVD presence/absence), dependent (tokens, iterations, accuracy, time, consistency), controlled (task complexity, model, prompt style)"
        - "Statistical tools: paired comparisons, confidence intervals, effect sizes (Cohen's d); explain each in accessible terms for a developer audience"
        - "Sample sizes and repetition: why running each experiment multiple times matters; how many trials are 'enough' for significance"
        - "Threats to validity: author bias (designing experiments to confirm hypotheses), task selection bias, model-specific results, generalizability limits"
        - "Reproducibility: all experiments are documented with methodology, data, and code so readers can replicate"
        - "How to read Chapters 12–16: each follows the same structure (hypothesis → experiment design → execution → results → analysis → implications)"
        - "Bridge to Chapter 12: 'The framework is set. Chapter 12 runs the first experiment: Context Efficiency.'"
  
  manuscript:
    file: "chapter.md"
    required_elements:
      - "Key Points or Summary block at end (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
      - "Key Points reflect book_system_intent Ch11 key_points: five validation categories, baseline vs IVD, statistical significance"
      - "Clear description of all five validation categories with hypotheses and measurement approaches"
      - "Experimental design pattern explained with enough detail for a developer to understand and replicate"
      -

analysis → implications)"
        - "Bridge to Chapter 12: 'The framework is set. Chapter 12 runs the first experiment: Context Efficiency.'"
  
  manuscript:
    file: "chapter.md"
    required_elements:
      - "Key Points or Summary block at end (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
      - "Key Points reflect book_system_intent Ch11 key_points: five validation categories, baseline vs IVD, statistical significance"
      - "Clear description of all five validation categories with hypotheses and measurement approaches"
      - "Experimental design pattern explained with enough detail for a developer to understand and replicate"
      - "Visual or structured overview: table or diagram mapping claims → categories → chapters"
      - "1–2 quotable lines where natural (constraint: quotable_principles)"
      - "Clear bridge to Chapter 12 (context efficiency experiments)"

# ==============================================================================
# KEY POINTS (from book_system_intent Ch11; must be reflected in chapter)
# ==============================================================================

key_points:
  - "How to measure IVD effectiveness scientifically — transition from illustrative measurements (Part II) to controlled experiments (Part III)"
  - "Five validation categories: context efficiency (token reduction), alignment accuracy (iteration reduction), knowledge durability (retention after reset), recipe effectiveness (time savings), meta-validation (self-governance)"
  - "Baseline vs IVD: controlled experiments with the same tasks, same models, measured outcomes — the experimental pattern all five categories share"
  - "Statistical significance and confidence intervals — the rigor standard that separates evidence from anecdote"
  - "This chapter defines the validation framework (methodology); Chapters 12–16 execute it and present results"
  - "Each validation category has a clear hypothesis, measurement approach, and baseline design"
  - "Threats to validity acknowledged: author bias, task selection, model specificity, generalizability limits"
  - "Reproducibility: every experiment documented so readers can replicate and verify"

# ==============================================================================
# CONSTRAINTS (from book intent; chapter-specific)
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "chapter_takeaways"
    requirement: "Chapter ends with 'Key Points' or 'Summary' block summarizing the validation framework and five categories"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a ## Key Points or ## Summary section"
    test: "grep '## Key Points\\|## Summary' chapter.md returns at least 1 match"
  
  - name: "quotable_principles"
    requirement: "1–2 quotable, memorable lines in chapter where natural"
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.quotable_lines"
    validation: "Manual: at least one line is tweetable/memorable"
    test: "At least one bold standalone sentence in chapter.md works as a tweet/pull-quote"
  
  - name: "writing_style_part_3"
    requirement: "Prose leads; evidence and methodo

"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a ## Key Points or ## Summary section"
    test: "grep '## Key Points\\|## Summary' chapter.md returns at least 1 match"
  
  - name: "quotable_principles"
    requirement: "1–2 quotable, memorable lines in chapter where natural"
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.quotable_lines"
    validation: "Manual: at least one line is tweetable/memorable"
    test: "At least one bold standalone sentence in chapter.md works as a tweet/pull-quote"
  
  - name: "writing_style_part_3"
    requirement: "Prose leads; evidence and methodology illustrations support. 'Prove this' drives learning. NOT code centerpiece (Part II style)."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.code_and_prose.part_1_3_4"
    validation: "Manual: prose explains and generalizes; examples/diagrams/tables illustrate; reader is understanding methodology, not building code"
    test: "chapter.md is primarily prose with illustrative tables/diagrams/examples; no section is led by a code block without preceding explanatory prose"
  
  - name: "formatting_conventions"
    requirement: "Follow book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions: bold for key terms, italic for emphasis, code for paths/commands, paragraph structure."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions"
    validation: "Manual: formatting matches conventions"
    test: "Key terms bolded on first use; code font for file paths and commands; no paragraph exceeds 10 sentences without subheading"
  
  - name: "evidence_based"
    requirement: "All claims in this chapter must be stated as hypotheses with defined methodology, not as established facts. This chapter defines what to measure and how — it does not assert results."
    source: "book_system_intent.part_3_validation.purpose"
    validation: "Every claim is framed as a hypothesis ('IVD reduces X by Y%') with methodology, not as proven fact"
    test: "No section states IVD results as fact without 'hypothesis,' 'we expect,' 'the experiment will measure,' or equivalent framing; grep for definitive claims ('IVD reduces,' 'IVD achieves') outside of hypothesis framing returns 0 matches"
  
  - name: "master_intent_alignment"
    requirement: "Chapter content must not contradict ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.master_intent_alignment"
    validation: "Manual review: Ch11 aligns with framework principles and Part III purpose; no contradictions"
    test: "No principle, validation category, or framework concept in chapter.md contradicts ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"
  
  - name: "no_timeline_references"
    requirement: "Zero timeline references in chapter content (days, weeks, months) unless in examples or experimental methodology"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_timeline_references"
    validation: "grep -i 'days|weeks|months|timeline' chapter.md (excluding methodology/examples) returns no matches"
    test: "rg -i '\\b(days|weeks|months|timeline)\\b' chapter.md outside fenced code blocks and methodology descriptions returns 0 match

ns"
    test: "No principle, validation category, or framework concept in chapter.md contradicts ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"
  
  - name: "no_timeline_references"
    requirement: "Zero timeline references in chapter content (days, weeks, months) unless in examples or experimental methodology"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_timeline_references"
    validation: "grep -i 'days|weeks|months|timeline' chapter.md (excluding methodology/examples) returns no matches"
    test: "rg -i '\\b(days|weeks|months|timeline)\\b' chapter.md outside fenced code blocks and methodology descriptions returns 0 matches"
  
  - name: "no_unsourced_statistics"
    requirement: "All numerical claims (85–95%, 40%, 80–90%) must be framed as hypotheses to be tested, not as proven results. No statistics presented as fact without experimental backing."
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_unsourced_statistics"
    validation: "Every percentage or numerical claim is explicitly a hypothesis or target, not a result"
    test: "All numerical claims in chapter.md are within hypothesis framing (e.g., 'we hypothesize,' 'the target is,' 'the experiment will test whether'); no number is presented as a proven result"

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Chapter 11 opens Part III by defining the validation framework — five categories, experimental methodology, statistical rigor. It defines HOW we prove IVD works; Chapters 12–16 execute the experiments and present results."
  
  reasoning: |
    - Part III purpose: prove every claim with data. This requires a framework chapter first — the reader needs to understand the methodology before seeing results.
    - Five validation categories chosen because they cover IVD's core value propositions: context efficiency (less data needed), alignment accuracy (fewer iterations), knowledge durability (survives resets), recipe effectiveness (reusable patterns), and meta-validation (self-governance). Together, they form a comprehensive evidence base.
    - Framework-first approach: defining methodology before presenting results follows scientific convention and builds reader trust. The reader knows HOW the evidence was gathered before seeing WHAT it shows.
    - Statistical rigor (significance, confidence intervals, effect sizes) differentiates this book from opinion-only books. Pattern: Code Complete's empirical chapters use data and studies; we do the same with IVD-specific experiments.
    - Baseline-vs-IVD pattern: every experiment uses the same structure (control vs. treatment) so the reader sees a consistent, reproducible approach.
    - Threats to validity acknowledged upfront: author bias, task selection, model specificity. This builds credibility by being honest about limitations.
  
  evidence: "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_3, Ch11 key_points, placeholder); Part II chapters (illustrative measurements); framework.md (IVD principles); Code Complete (empirical methodology pattern)"
  date: "2026-02-09"
  stakeholder: "IVD book

chapters use data and studies; we do the same with IVD-specific experiments.
    - Baseline-vs-IVD pattern: every experiment uses the same structure (control vs. treatment) so the reader sees a consistent, reproducible approach.
    - Threats to validity acknowledged upfront: author bias, task selection, model specificity. This builds credibility by being honest about limitations.
  
  evidence: "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_3, Ch11 key_points, placeholder); Part II chapters (illustrative measurements); framework.md (IVD principles); Code Complete (empirical methodology pattern)"
  date: "2026-02-09"
  stakeholder: "IVD book author"

# ==============================================================================
# ALTERNATIVES
# ==============================================================================

alternatives:
  - name: "Skip the framework chapter; present results directly in Ch11–16"
    rejected_because: "Without a methodology chapter, the reader has no context for evaluating results. They don't know what's being measured, how, or why. Results without framework look like cherry-picked anecdotes."
    when_it_works: "If the audience is already familiar with experimental methodology (academic audience). Our audience is developers who need the methodology explained."
  
  - name: "Combine framework and first experiment (context efficiency) into one chapter"
    rejected_because: "Too dense. The framework itself — five categories, experimental design, statistical tools, threats to validity — needs its own space. Combining it with the first experiment would force either shallow methodology or a 60+ page chapter."
    when_it_works: "If there were only 2–3 validation categories and the methodology were simpler."

# ==============================================================================
# RISKS
# ==============================================================================

risks:
  - condition: "Reader finds the methodology chapter too academic or dry (no results yet)"
    action: "Keep prose engaging with the 'why this matters' motivation for each category; use concrete examples from Part II projects as previews; end each category section with a compelling question that Chapter 12–16 will answer"
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback: 'I skipped Ch11' or 'too theoretical'"
    severity: "high"
  
  - condition: "Statistical concepts (significance, confidence intervals, effect sizes) alienate developer readers"
    action: "Explain in developer-friendly terms (avoid jargon where possible); use analogies (confidence interval ≈ error bars on a benchmark); keep explanations practical, not mathematical"
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback: 'I didn't understand the statistics section'"
    severity: "medium"
  
  - condition: "Hypothesized numbers (85–95%, 40%, 80–90%) set expectations too high and actual results in Ch12–16 don't match"
    action: "Frame all numbers as hypotheses to be tested, not promises. Use language like 'we expect' and 'the experiment will determine.' Ch12–16 present actual results honestly, even if lower than hypothesized."
    monitor: "Cross-check with Ch12–16 results during manuscript review; update hy

nalogies (confidence interval ≈ error bars on a benchmark); keep explanations practical, not mathematical"
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback: 'I didn't understand the statistics section'"
    severity: "medium"
  
  - condition: "Hypothesized numbers (85–95%, 40%, 80–90%) set expectations too high and actual results in Ch12–16 don't match"
    action: "Frame all numbers as hypotheses to be tested, not promises. Use language like 'we expect' and 'the experiment will determine.' Ch12–16 present actual results honestly, even if lower than hypothesized."
    monitor: "Cross-check with Ch12–16 results during manuscript review; update hypotheses if needed"
    severity: "high"
  
  - condition: "Chapter feels disconnected from Part II practice chapters — reader doesn't see the bridge"
    action: "Section 11.1 explicitly bridges Part II → Part III: 'In Part II you measured illustrative improvements. Part III formalizes those measurements into controlled experiments.' Reference specific Part II projects and their measurements."
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback on Part II → Part III transition"
    severity: "medium"

# ==============================================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION
# ==============================================================================

implementation:
  intent_artifact: "manuscript/part-3-validation/chapter-11-validation-framework/chapter_11_validation_framework_intent.yaml"
  output: "manuscript/part-3-validation/chapter-11-validation-framework/chapter.md"
  status: "pending"
  version: 1
  
  process:
    reference: "book/chapter_writing_process_intent.yaml"
    current_step: "step_3_create_chapter_intent"
    completed_steps:
      - "step_1_verify_prerequisites"
      - "step_2_create_chapter_folder"
      - "step_3_create_chapter_intent"
    pending_steps:
      - "step_4_write_manuscript"
      - "step_5_cross_check"
      - "step_6_update_chapter_status"
      - "step_7_update_book_intent"

# ==============================================================================

    - check: "Experimental design pattern (baseline vs IVD) clearly explained with variables, statistical tools, and threats to validity"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[11.7]"
    - check: "All numerical claims framed as hypotheses, not proven results"
      validates: "constraints.evidence_based, constraints.no_unsourced_statistics"
    - check: "Writing style: prose leads; evidence and methodology illustrate (P

validates: "constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    - check: "All five validation categories are defined with hypothesis, measurement approach, and baseline design"

# ==============================================================================
# CHANGELOG
# ==============================================================================

changelog:
  - version: "1.0"
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: |
      Initial chapter intent for Chapter 11 'The Validation Framework'.
      Derived from book_system_intent.yaml Ch11 (key_points, placeholder), Part III purpose
      (prove every claim with data), writing_style.code_and_prose.part_1_3_4 (prose leads),
      and Code Complete's empirical methodology pattern.
      Sections 11.1–11.7 with required_content, constraints, rationale, alternatives, risks, verification.
      Part III writing style applied (prose leads, evidence illustrates).
      Evidence-based constraint added: all claims framed as hypotheses with methodology.
      No recipe_alignment or author_sourced_examples (Part III defines methodology, not projects).
      Risks identified: dry methodology, statistics alienation, hypothesized numbers, Part II disconnect.
    reason: "IVD: one intent per chapter; Ch11 opens Part III with the validation framework. Intent-only; manuscript pending."
